data.title: Japanese stock index futures rose 10%, and the rise in Nikkei average VI futures triggered the circuit breaker mechanism
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Nikkei 225 Index futures rose 10% in early trading, and Nikkei Average VI futures were suspended due to triggering the circuit breaker mechanism. (Jinshi)

data.title: US stocks closed: Trump postponed trade policy, Nasdaq surged 12%
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Trump eased the implementation of reciprocal trade measures, and the three major U.S. stock indexes soared. The Dow Jones Industrial Average initially closed up 7.87%, the S&P 500 rose 9.51%, and the Nasdaq Composite Index rose 12.16%. Tesla (TSLA.O) rose 22%, Apple (AAPL.O) rose 15%, and Nvidia (NVDA.O) rose 18%. Among blockchain concept stocks, MARA Holdings (MARA) rose 17.02%, Strategy (MSTR.O) rose 24.76%, and Coinbase (COIN.O) rose 16.91%.
U.S. President Trump said: The record performance of the stock market should continue. (Jinshi)

data.title: The U.S. CFTC has clearly stopped regulating the crypto asset industry through prosecution
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: Caroline D. Pham, Acting Chair of the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), expressed support for the Department of Justice's announcement to end the policy of regulating the crypto asset industry through prosecution, and instructed the CFTC not to bring lawsuits for violating the registration provisions of the Commodity Exchange Act in digital asset-related cases without the defendant's subjective intent. 
In addition, she has adjusted the functions of the law enforcement department to promote the rapid resolution of compliance backlogs that do not involve customer damage or market abuse.

data.title: CFTC Chair Supports DOJ's New Crypto Regulation Approach
data.description:
According to Odaily, Caroline D. Pham, the acting chair of the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), has expressed support for the Department of Justice's decision to end the policy of regulating the crypto asset industry through prosecutions. She has instructed the CFTC not to file lawsuits in digital asset cases based on violations of the Commodity Exchange Act's registration provisions unless there is evidence of the defendant's subjective intent.

Additionally, Pham has restructured the enforcement department's functions to expedite the resolution of compliance cases that do not involve customer harm or market abuse.

data.title: White House: Tariff levels will be reduced to a general 10% during negotiations
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Trump posted on social media that in view of the fact that more than 75 countries have called the United States representative agencies to negotiate solutions to issues related to trade, trade barriers, tariffs, exchange rate manipulation and non-monetary tariffs, I have approved a 90-day suspension of these countries. The suspension applies to reciprocal tariffs. During this period, the general tariff will be reduced to 10%, and the suspension will take effect immediately. The 90-day tariff suspension does not apply to US tariffs on Mexico and Canada.
A White House spokesman said that tailored negotiations will continue, and the tariff level will be reduced to a general 10% during the negotiations. The White House posted on x that it will suspend for 90 days and reduce the reciprocal tariffs on most countries to 10%, effective immediately.

data.title: Whale Sells Off Virtual Tokens at Significant Loss
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, a significant investor recently sold off 5.049 million VIRTUAL tokens, valued at $2.28 million, incurring substantial losses. This marks the second time the investor has faced losses with VIRTUAL tokens, totaling $8.4 million.

In January, the investor purchased 5.038 million VIRTUAL tokens for $13.91 million at a price of $2.76 per token. By early February, the tokens were sold at $1.76 each, resulting in a $5.02 million loss. Undeterred, the investor bought 5.049 million VIRTUAL tokens again at the end of February for $5.66 million, priced at $1.12 per token. Six hours ago, these tokens were sold off at $0.45 each, leading to an additional loss of $3.38 million.

data.title: Nikkei 225 Futures Surge 10% Amid Trading Suspension
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the Nikkei 225 index futures experienced a 10% increase during early trading. Meanwhile, trading of Nikkei Average VI futures was halted due to the activation of a circuit breaker mechanism.

data.title: US SEC approves options trading for spot Ethereum ETF
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to market news, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission has now approved options trading for spot Ethereum ETFs.

data.title: Federal Reserve's May Rate Decision: 76.1% Chance of No Change
data.description: According to PANews, the CME's 'FedWatch' tool indicates a 76.1% probability that the Federal Reserve will maintain its current interest rates in May. There is a 23.9% chance of a 25 basis point rate cut. Looking ahead to June, the likelihood of rates remaining unchanged is 32.1%, while the probability of a cumulative 25 basis point cut stands at 54.1%, and a 50 basis point cut is at 13.8%.

data.title: Pakistan considers using surplus energy for Bitcoin mining after appointing CZ to Crypto Council
data.description:
Pakistan will allocate excess electricity from its national grid to Bitcoin (BTC) mining operations and artificial intelligence (AI) data centers, according to the country’s Crypto Council and finance ministry adviser Bilal Bin Saqib.
source: https://cryptoslate.com/pakistan-considers-using-surplus-energy-for-bitcoin-mining-after-appointing-cz-to-crypto-council/
